event: Portrait of Saint Stephen the Deacon holding a stone
symbols: stone held in hand — the instrument of Stephen's martyrdom by stoning
deacon's dalmatic — Stephen's office as one of the first seven deacons
youthful face — innocence and the beauty of faithful witness
city in background — Jerusalem, where Stephen was martyred
calm expression — the serenity of one who sees heaven opened
description: A tall, narrow altarpiece panel showing a young man (Saint Stephen) standing in a landscape, wearing an ornate brown and gold dalmatic vestment over a white alb. He holds a smooth stone in his raised left hand — the attribute of his martyrdom. His face is youthful with reddish-brown hair, gazing to the side with a serene expression. In the background, a winding path leads to a city with buildings and towers, and figures are visible in the distance. The top of the panel is arched.
theological_description: Saint Stephen, the first Christian martyr (protomartyr), was one of the seven deacons chosen to serve the early Church (Acts 6:5). His powerful preaching and the signs he performed aroused the fury of certain synagogue members who brought him before the Sanhedrin on false charges of blasphemy (Acts 6:11-14). Stephen's defense — a sweeping survey of Israel's history of rejecting God's messengers — culminated in his accusation: 'You always resist the Holy Spirit!' (Acts 7:51). As they raged against him, Stephen 'full of the Holy Spirit, looked up to heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing at the right hand of God' (Acts 7:55). His death by stoning (Acts 7:58-60) echoed Christ's own Passion: like Jesus, Stephen prayed for his killers — 'Lord, do not hold this sin against them' (Acts 7:60; cf. Luke 23:34). The stone he holds is both the instrument of his death and a badge of honor — the first in the long line of those who followed the Lamb 'faithful unto death' (Revelation 2:10). Saul of Tarsus witnessed Stephen's martyrdom (Acts 7:58), and the seed planted by Stephen's blood bore fruit in Paul's conversion.

scripture_refs.reason: Stephen's vision of heaven and his death by stoning — the stone in his hand is the direct attribute
Stephen chosen as one of the seven deacons — his vestment identifies his office
They stoned Stephen while he prayed — the foundational event of his martyrdom
Stephen's prayer for his killers echoes Christ's 'Father forgive them'
Be faithful unto death and receive the crown of life — Stephen as the first to fulfill this call
